authoritative_sources.title: CMMC Model Overview v2
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines CMMC framework scope and evidence expectations that directly influence how defense industrial operations classify and sequence control-related work.

authoritative_sources.title: CMMC Scoping Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines what systems and evidence families belong inside CMMC operational scope for defense environments, preventing overgeneralization into generic operations guidance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Request intake and evidence boundary definition

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.204-7012, Safeguarding Covered Defense Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides obligations for safeguarding covered defense information and cyber incident reporting that affect completion evidence and escalation criteria.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-171 R2, Protecting CUI in Nonfederal Systems
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Standard controls baseline used by many CMMC implementations; required for structured evidence readiness in mission-support workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-172, Enhanced Security Requirements for CUI
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines elevated controls for high-impact protected environments and informs when lane must escalate rather than close a case.

authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 5000.02, Operation of the Defense Acquisition System
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines defense lifecycle governance and authority boundaries used for in-lane handoffs and escalation ownership.

authoritative_sources.title: NAICS sector guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official industry classification context used to constrain this pack to defense industrial base and mission systems rather than generic cyber ops.

workflow_stages.name: Intake and scope validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate facility, mission, and request scope against CMMC boundary guidance before any state progression.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence assembly and sufficiency checks
workflow_stages.description: Collect facility and control artifacts, confirm tenant approvals, and determine whether evidence supports a safe progression.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception and rework handling
workflow_stages.description: Escalate or route low-confidence and incomplete cases with explicit blockers rather than proceeding through completion.

workflow_stages.name: Completion and handoff
workflow_stages.description: Emit completion evidence and completion-disposition package only when dependencies, approvals, and safety gates are proven.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Conflict and source governance
workflow_stages.description: Detect conflicts between public sources and tenant policy and escalate instead of forcing confident conclusions.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Facility and mission operation scope statement
System access and IAM review artifacts
Incident and readiness logs from operations systems
Completion evidence checklist and handoff package
systems_of_record: Security and CUI control evidence repositories
Policy, compliance, and approval authority directories

allowed: Progress only when scope, evidence sufficiency, and authority context are verified.
Escalate unresolved authority conflicts and legal/safety/finance requests outside lane scope.
Normalize mission terms to artifacts before state transition or completion claims.
escalate: Tenant mission-policy conflict with public source posture
Missing control evidence for completion-stage transitions
refuse: Requests for legal waiver authority or policy override
Direct safety certification or final mission-release decisions without required owners
Completion claims without approved evidence and approval logs

domain_failure_modes: Confusing mission terminology with generic operational vocabulary and skipping tenant mapping.
Completing work without complete evidence traceability across systems-of-record.
Routing to safety/legal/financial owners without explicit authority context.
Allowing source-policy conflicts to be interpreted as complete and progressing anyway.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant definition of mission-essential versus non-mission-critical systems
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Different tenants may classify assets differently, which materially changes progression and evidence requirements.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant mission dictionary and classification map at runtime; escalate if missing before completion.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Sufficiency threshold for evidence retention and retention-window
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Evidence retention windows may vary by tenant policy and mission contract.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat retention and timing as mandatory tenant inputs; never infer from public baseline alone.

notes: Source mix covers defense operating guidance, CMMC baseline standards, control evidence, and industry classification.
Workflow stages include intake, evidence, exception, completion, and conflict handling with explicit safe escalation.
No blocking unresolved ambiguities remain because each ambiguity is converted into required tenant input or escalation requirement before completion.
